switchgear.rule: Count every switchgear/distribution item individually. Always note current rating, phases, and number of ways/outgoers. Use equipment schedule if available.

main_switchboard.name: Main LV Switchboard / Main Switch Panel (MSP)
main_switchboard.function: Primary LV distribution point for the building. Receives incoming supply from transformer or utility and distributes to sub-main circuits.
specification_required: Form of separation (Form 1/2/3/4 per BS EN 61439)
IP rating (IP31 standard internal, IP54 external)

form_of_separation.form_1: No separation between busbars, functional units, or terminals. Basic.
form_of_separation.form_2: Separation of busbars from functional units. Terminals not separated.
form_of_separation.form_3: Separation of busbars, functional units, AND terminals from each other.
form_of_separation.form_4: As Form 3, plus separation of terminals of functional units from each other. Highest separation.
form_of_separation.note: Higher form number = safer for maintenance but more expensive. Form 2 is standard commercial. Form 4 for critical/hospital.

distribution_board.name: Distribution Board (DB)
distribution_board.function: Local distribution of circuits to final loads (lighting, sockets, equipment). Fed from main switchboard via sub-main cable.

three_phase.name: Three Phase Distribution Board (TP&N)
three_phase.use: Standard commercial distribution. Each way has an MCB or RCBO.

single_phase.name: Single Phase Distribution Board (SP&N)
single_phase.use: Small commercial, residential, dedicated single-phase loads.

naming_convention.note: DBs are typically named by floor and function:

flag_if: Protection type (MCB vs RCBO) not stated

consumer_unit.name: Consumer Unit (CU)
consumer_unit.function: Domestic/small commercial distribution board. Contains main switch and MCBs/RCBOs.
consumer_unit.standard: BS 7671 Amendment 3 requires all consumer units in domestic premises to be non-combustible (metal enclosure)
consumer_unit.note: Since BS 7671 18th Edition, ALL consumer units in domestic premises must be metal-clad (not plastic). Check if this applies.
flag_if: Plastic consumer unit specified for domestic — flag as potentially non-compliant with Amendment 3

mcc.name: Motor Control Centre (MCC)
mcc.function: Centralised panel for controlling multiple motors — starters, VFDs, protection

capacitor_bank.name: Capacitor Bank / Power Factor Correction (PFC) Panel
capacitor_bank.function: Corrects power factor to avoid reactive power charges from utility. Reduces current draw for same real power.
capacitor_bank.note: Only required for larger installations where power factor is an issue (typically >100kVA). Check if utility imposes reactive power charges.
flag_if: Large installation (>200kVA) without PFC shown — flag for spec check

mccb_panel.name: MCCB Panel / MCCB Distribution Board
mccb_panel.function: Distribution using Moulded Case Circuit Breakers instead of MCBs. For higher current outgoers (>125A per way).
mccb_panel.note: Used between main switchboard and distribution boards. Each MCCB feeds a sub-main cable to a DB or group of loads.

transformers.rule: Count every transformer. Note kVA rating and primary/secondary voltages. HV transformers are SPECIALIST SCOPE — always flag.

hv_lv.name: HV/LV Transformer
hv_lv.function: Steps down high voltage (11kV or 6.6kV) to low voltage (400V). Located in transformer room or external compound.

types.oil_filled: Traditional. External installation or in fire-rated transformer room with bund for oil containment.
types.cast_resin: Dry type. Can be installed inside building without bund. Preferred for internal/commercial.
types.note: Cast resin transformers are increasingly specified for new commercial buildings — no oil spill risk.

hv_lv.flag_reason: HV/LV transformer installation involves DNO (Distribution Network Operator), specialist HV contractor, and building control approval. Always flag as specialist scope.

step_down.name: Step-Down Transformer (LV/LV)
step_down.function: Steps down LV voltage — e.g. 400V to 230V for dedicated loads, or 400V to 110V for site power.
common_applications: 110V supply for construction site power tools

isolating.name: Isolating Transformer
isolating.function: Provides galvanic isolation between primary and secondary. Safety requirement for certain applications.
specification_required: Application (bathroom zone, medical IT system, etc.)
isolating.note: Medical IT systems (IEC 61557-8) require isolating transformer + insulation monitoring device (IMD). Both are nr items.

standby_power.rule: Count every standby power item. Note kVA rating and autonomy/runtime. These are high-value items with significant associated works.

generator.name: Standby Generator (Diesel/Gas)
generator.function: Provides electrical power during mains failure. Diesel is standard. Gas for CHP or continuous-rated.
specification_required: Autonomy (hours at full load — depends on fuel tank size)
associated_items: Exhaust flue (m linear + terminal + acoustic attenuation)
Combustion air intake and discharge louvres

sizes.large: 500-2000 kVA — large commercial, hospital, data centre
sizes.note: Generator size is determined by essential load analysis — not total building load.

ups.name: UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply)
ups.function: Provides continuous power to critical loads during mains failure — bridges gap until generator starts or for short outages.

types.online_double: Online double conversion — continuous power conditioning. All power through inverter. Standard for critical loads.
types.line_interactive: Line-interactive — simpler, lower cost. Suitable for IT equipment, non-critical.
types.static: Static UPS — large centralized system. Data centres, hospitals.
types.rotary: Rotary UPS — flywheel-based. Long runtime without batteries. Specialist.

specification_required: Autonomy at full load (minutes — typically 5, 10, 15, 20, 30 min)
Battery type (VRLA sealed lead-acid standard, lithium emerging)
associated_items: Battery cabinet/rack (may be separate from UPS)
Ventilation for battery room (hydrogen venting for VRLA)
flag_if: No maintenance bypass shown for UPS >10kVA

ats.name: ATS (Automatic Transfer Switch)
ats.function: Automatically switches load from mains to generator (and back) during power failure.

types.open_transition: Break-before-make — brief interruption during transfer. Standard for most applications.
types.closed_transition: Make-before-break — momentary parallel. For sensitive loads that cannot tolerate any break.
types.soft_load: Closed transition with programmable load transfer ramping.

ats.note: ATS is shown between mains incomer and generator on single-line diagram. Essential loads downstream of ATS, non-essential loads upstream.

battery_bank.name: Battery Bank / Battery System
battery_bank.function: Energy storage for UPS, emergency lighting central battery, or DC systems
battery_bank.note: Battery rooms require ventilation to prevent hydrogen accumulation (VRLA batteries). BS EN 50272-2 applies. Check building regulations for battery room requirements.

metering.rule: Count every meter and monitoring device. Utility meters are typically BY OTHERS — flag scope boundary.

utility_meter.name: Electricity Meter (Utility / Fiscal)
utility_meter.function: Measures total building electricity consumption. Owned by utility company.
utility_meter.flag_reason: Utility meter is supplied and installed by the electricity utility company (UKPN, WPD, SSE, etc.). M&E contractor provides the metering cabinet and CT chamber. Confirm scope split.
associated_items: CT chamber (by M&E if HV metering)
utility_meter.note: Lead time for utility metering connection can be 12-20 weeks. Flag for programme.

sub_metering.name: Sub-Metering / AMR (Automatic Meter Reading)
sub_metering.function: Measures energy consumption of individual circuits, floors, or tenants for billing or monitoring.

types.ct_meter: CT-operated energy meter — clamps around cable, non-invasive. Most common.
types.direct_meter: Direct-connected meter — for smaller loads (<100A). Inline with circuit.
types.pulse_meter: Pulse output meter — sends pulses to BMS/AMR for remote reading.

specification_required: Communication method (pulse, Modbus, BACnet, M-Bus, wireless)
associated_items: Current transformers (CTs) — typically 3nr per three-phase circuit metered
flag_if: Number of metered circuits not stated
Part L compliance metering not addressed (mandatory for new non-domestic buildings)

power_monitoring.name: Power Monitoring Unit (PMU) / Power Quality Meter
power_monitoring.function: Advanced monitoring of power quality — voltage, current, power factor, harmonics, THD
power_monitoring.note: PMUs are typically installed in main switchboards and critical distribution boards. More expensive than basic sub-meters.

current_transformers.name: Current Transformers (CTs)
current_transformers.function: Transforms high current to measurable low current for metering. Required for every metered circuit.
current_transformers.count_rule: Count 3nr CTs per three-phase metered circuit, 1nr CT per single-phase metered circuit.
specification_required: Accuracy class (0.5 for billing, 1.0 for monitoring)
Type (split-core for retrofit, solid-core for new)
current_transformers.note: CTs are small items but there can be many of them. 10 metered three-phase circuits = 30 CTs. Count carefully.

hv_equipment.flag_reason: ALL HV equipment is SPECIALIST SCOPE. Requires: specialist HV contractor, DNO (Distribution Network Operator) involvement, HV authorised persons, specific safety procedures. M&E estimator should note HV equipment but flag that pricing requires specialist input.

ring_main_unit.name: Ring Main Unit (RMU)
ring_main_unit.function: HV switching point — connects building to DNO ring main. Typically 11kV.
ring_main_unit.note: RMU is often supplied by DNO and installed in a purpose-built substation room provided by the developer.

hv_switchgear.name: HV Switchgear / HV Circuit Breaker
hv_switchgear.function: Protection and isolation on HV circuits
hv_switchgear.note: Specialist — price from HV contractor quotation.

hv_cable.name: HV Cable
hv_cable.function: Carries high voltage power from DNO network to building transformer
hv_cable.note: HV cable installation requires specialist jointing (heat-shrink terminations) and testing (HV insulation testing). NOT standard M&E scope.
hv_cable.flag_if: Any HV cable shown — flag as specialist scope.

dno_interface.note: DNO (Distribution Network Operator) involvement is required for:
items: New HV supply connection or upgrade
uk_dnos: WPD / National Grid (South West, Midlands, Wales)
dno_interface.lead_time: 12-52 weeks depending on scope. ALWAYS flag lead time risk for HV works.

earthing.rule: Earthing is MANDATORY for every electrical installation per BS 7671. Flag if no earthing strategy is shown on drawings or specified.

earth_electrode.name: Earth Electrode / Ground Rod
earth_electrode.function: Provides a physical connection to earth (soil). Required for TT earthing systems and supplementary earthing.

types.driven_rod: Copper-clad steel rod driven into ground. Standard: 1.2m, 2.4m, or 3.0m sections. Multiple sections coupled for depth.
types.earth_mat: Buried copper mesh/tape — for larger installations, substations.
types.foundation_earth: Copper tape/cable embedded in building foundations during construction.

specification_required: Target earth resistance (Ω — typically <10Ω, <1Ω for substations)
earth_electrode.count_rule: Count every electrode. Note type and target resistance. Rods may need multiple sections to achieve required resistance.
associated: Earth cable from electrode to earth bar

earth_bar.name: Main Earth Bar / Earth Terminal
earth_bar.function: Central connection point for all earthing conductors in the building or section.

types.main_earth_terminal: MET — main earth terminal. One per building. Connection point for utility earth, electrode, and main protective conductors.
types.supplementary_earth_bar: SEB — local earth bar in switchroom, plant room, or riser. Connects local earthing to main earth system.

earth_bar.count_rule: Count every earth bar. Typically: 1nr MET + 1nr SEB per switchroom/plant room.
earth_bar.note: Earth bars are mounted on insulated standoffs. Include mounting brackets and insulated supports.

earth_cable.name: Earth Cable / Protective Conductor

types.main_protective: Main protective conductor — from MET to main switchboard and to earth electrode. Green/yellow PVC or bare copper.
types.supplementary_bonding: Supplementary bonding conductors — connecting exposed metalwork in zones (bathrooms, plant rooms).
types.circuit_cpc: Circuit protective conductor — within each circuit cable (usually integral). Not separately measured unless singles in conduit.

typical_sizes.120mm²: Main earth conductor (very large installation / substation)

earth_cable.note: Earth cable sizes are determined by BS 7671 calculations based on fault current. If not stated on drawings, flag for design check.

main_bonding.name: Main Protective Bonding
main_bonding.function: Connects extraneous-conductive-parts (gas pipe, water pipe, structural steel) to MET within 600mm of building entry.
items_to_bond: Other metallic services entering the building
main_bonding.count_rule: Count 1nr bonding connection per service entering the building. Note conductor size and clamp type.

supplementary_bonding.name: Supplementary Bonding
supplementary_bonding.function: Connects exposed metalwork within a zone to local earth — bathrooms, plant rooms, swimming pools.
supplementary_bonding.count_rule: Count 1nr bonding clamp per connection point. Can be many in a large plant room.
supplementary_bonding.note: BS 7671 18th Edition reduced supplementary bonding requirements in bathrooms IF main bonding and RCD protection is in place. Check spec.

clamp_types.note: Count clamps as nr items. State size range (pipe diameter or steel thickness).

flag_triggers: No earthing strategy shown on drawings — flag as CRITICAL. Earthing is mandatory per BS 7671.
No main earth bar (MET) shown — flag.
No earth electrode shown and earthing system type (TN-S, TN-C-S, TT) not stated — flag.
No main bonding shown to gas, water, steel — flag.
Plant room shown without supplementary bonding — flag.
Bathroom/wet area without supplementary bonding assessment — flag for review.

earthing_systems.TN-S: Separate neutral and earth from utility. Earth via utility cable sheath. Most reliable.
earthing_systems.TN-C-S: Combined neutral/earth (PME) from utility, separated at building intake. Common in UK.
earthing_systems.TT: No earth from utility — building must provide its own earth electrode. Common in rural areas.
earthing_systems.note: Earthing system type affects: electrode requirements, fault loop impedance, RCD selection. Check with DNO or incoming supply details.
